02a · MAISI latent-encoding fix — verification

Fix: dynamic_infer (whole-volume if the volume fits the ROI, else ROI clamped to the volume — never pad beyond the input). Applied in encode_all_volumes.py and trainer._encode_sliding_window, ROI [320,320,160] (matches NV-Generate-CTMR).
Result: brain decode(encode(GT)) soft-tissue error is now -26 HU on average (was +39…+85 with the oversized-ROI padding). No brightening.

This is the VAE round-trip on ground-truth CT with the patched encoder (the exact function used to regenerate the training latents). It is the model's achievable ceiling per region.

regionsubjinput shapelatent shapeGT soft medrecon soft medsoft err [HU]
brain (train)1BA001(160, 192, 160)(40, 48, 40)33-4-35
brain1BB005(160, 192, 160)(40, 48, 40)348-27
brain1BB006(160, 160, 128)(40, 40, 32)3523-12
brain1BC001(160, 192, 128)(40, 48, 32)31-4-32
head-neck1HNC007(192, 192, 160)(48, 48, 40)33-4-36
thorax1THB006(320, 320, 256)(80, 80, 64)31-12-40
pelvis1PC001(352, 256, 160)(88, 64, 40)-57-66-13
abdomen1ABB002(320, 320, 288)(80, 80, 72)-26-51-29

Brain at the clinical [-100,100] HU window

The window that washed out white before. With the fixed encode, brain reconstructs as mid-gray — the residual is a mild ~−16…−30 HU softening (VAE compression), not the +85 HU brightening.

All regions [-100,150] HU

Mechanism note: the corrupted brightening was caused by the VAE encoder's 13 GroupNorm layers (decoder has 13) normalizing over a volume flooded with zero-padding; dynamic_infer removes the padding so the encoder GroupNorm sees only real anatomy.

Generated by ~/vae_audit/verify_and_02a.py. Encoder is variational (sampled), so values vary ~±a few HU per run. Companion to the retracted/​corrected 02_maisi_vae_brain_hu_audit.html.
